text.title: Compute and Database Rightsizing Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules grounding the finops-devops-cost-rightsizing methodology, each paired with rationale and source reference.

rule.statement: Drive rightsizing recommendations from P95 (not average) CPU + memory + IOPS over a 14-day window covering one business cycle.
rule.rationale: Average hides bursts; P95 captures sustained load and protects SLOs while shedding tail.

rule.statement: Downsize one instance class at a time and validate the post-change P95 utilisation lands between 50% and 70% before further reduction.
rule.rationale: Multi-step jumps trigger latency regression; the 50-70% landing zone leaves burst headroom.

rule.statement: For RDS / Cloud SQL, rightsize compute and storage jointly — drop to gp3 from gp2 and tune IOPS/throughput separately from disk size.
rule.rationale: gp2 ties IOPS to size; gp3 unbundles them and saves cost without provisioning extra disk for IOPS.

rule.statement: For x86 workloads on AWS, move stateless and JVM workloads to Graviton (ARM) families when language toolchain supports it.
rule.rationale: Graviton typically saves 20-40% at equal or better throughput for stateless and JVM workloads.

rule.statement: Schedule auto-stop for non-production instances outside business hours and validate that startup latency fits the dev workflow.
rule.rationale: Non-prod runs 168h/wk while devs use ~40h; scheduled stop captures the 76% idle window.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, do NOT run this methodology — return early with reason.
rule.rationale: Decision tree's negative branch must terminate cleanly without partial output.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold AND scope is explicit, run the methodology end-to-end and produce the canonical artefact.
rule.rationale: Decision tree positive-branch terminus; aliases the apply-end-to-end verdict.
